title: Lice

jokes.text: I bit my lip at lunch today. Sucks. Anyway, I just don't see it. The Knicks are tough.
jokes.setup: Jim appears to be making basketball analysis
jokes.punchline: His casual transition from lip injury to sports commentary shows he's distracted
jokes.explanation: Subtle character moment showing Jim's divided attention between personal issues and work conversation

jokes.text: Morning, Meredith. What? Oh, sorry. Pam.
jokes.setup: Pam greets who she thinks is Meredith
jokes.punchline: She's so tired she's confusing people, revealing her exhausted state
jokes.explanation: Efficient way to show Pam's exhaustion through a simple mistake

jokes.text: how would I feel if I was at home, stuck with the kids while she was go-karting with John Stamos?
jokes.setup: Jim trying to be empathetic about role reversal
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific John Stamos go-karting scenario
jokes.explanation: Beautifully absurd specificity that's both relatable and ridiculous

jokes.text: Jim lies about taking a taxi instead of a limo to his meeting
jokes.setup: Jim says his 'taxi' is here
jokes.punchline: We just heard someone mention his limo has arrived
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony showing Jim's guilt about living differently than Pam

jokes.text: Here's how you do it. You say, 'What are you gonna do, break up with me?' Like, as a joke. And then you gain a lot of weight.
jokes.setup: Darryl explaining his breakup strategy
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of intentional weight gain as relationship sabotage
jokes.explanation: Perfectly deadpan delivery of an absurd but believable self-sabotage method

jokes.text: No wonder Jim left you.
jokes.setup: Pam asking Meredith to fill out forms
jokes.punchline: Meredith's brutal and inappropriate response
jokes.explanation: Classic Meredith cruelty that lands because it hits Pam's insecurity

jokes.text: Not always like an ape, the way Meredith just did, but it happens.
jokes.setup: Pam trying to rationalize Meredith's scratching
jokes.punchline: Her specific description of ape-like scratching
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy description with Pam's desperate rationalization

jokes.text: Between the foster homes and the orphanage, I had lice 22 times.
jokes.setup: Meredith claiming expertise with lice
jokes.punchline: The casually dark backstory delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith moment - dark personal history delivered casually

jokes.text: Holy wow, that's a big one.
jokes.setup: Meredith examining someone's scalp for lice
jokes.punchline: Her impressed reaction to finding a large louse
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that works because of Meredith's genuine enthusiasm

jokes.text: For 15 years, they called me 'freak' and 'four eyes' and 'sci-fi nerd' and 'girl puncher,' all because I had lice when I was seven.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his traumatic lice experience
jokes.punchline: The list escalates from lice-related to completely unrelated insults
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with 'girl puncher' being completely unrelated to lice, perfect Dwight logic

jokes.text: I use lice shampoo every morning.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why his scalp is so clean
jokes.punchline: His daily preventative lice shampoo routine
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Dwight behavior that's totally believable for his character

jokes.text: It's not one of your bean-bag orgies.
jokes.setup: Angela criticizing Meredith's workplace behavior
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific 'bean-bag orgies' insult
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and judgmental Angela line that's both prudish and weirdly imaginative

jokes.text: Nobody's taking Meredith Palmer to the opera to meet the queen.
jokes.setup: Meredith responding to criticism about her hygiene
jokes.punchline: Her oddly specific scenario of opera and royalty
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware Meredith moment with absurd specificity

jokes.text: That stands for hazardous materials men's suit wearing.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about Dwight's hazmat suit
jokes.punchline: His completely wrong but confident explanation of 'hazmat'
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight confidently delivering misinformation

jokes.text: If you rent more than four times a year, it just makes sense to buy.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his hazmat suit ownership
jokes.punchline: His practical approach to hazmat suit economics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant because it applies rental car logic to hazmat suits, perfectly Dwight

jokes.text: If you don't hear from me by lunch, call me. I might want lunch.
jokes.setup: Dwight giving heroic last words before staying to fight lice
jokes.punchline: The anticlimatic reason he might want to be called
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection from heroic sacrifice to mundane hunger

jokes.text: The only thing that got me through mine was large amounts of Shepherd's pie and Brandy. The singer, not the drink.
jokes.setup: Kevin offering breakup advice about comfort food and alcohol
jokes.punchline: The clarification that he meant the singer Brandy
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke that feels authentically Kevin

jokes.text: There's a pencil poking in my rolls.
jokes.setup: Stanley reluctantly staying after Dwight's physical restraint
jokes.punchline: His specific complaint about the pencil placement
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff to Dwight's restraint with Stanley's matter-of-fact complaint

jokes.text: You know, I think these critters migrated from down south.
jokes.setup: Meredith heating up wax and discussing her lice problem
jokes.punchline: Her casual reference to pubic lice
jokes.explanation: Classic Meredith overshare that's gross but character-appropriate

jokes.text: follicle gypsies, hair lawyers
jokes.setup: Dwight dramatically describing the lice situation
jokes.punchline: His creative metaphorical names for lice
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully creative Dwight terminology that's both poetic and absurd

jokes.text: Sha-boom! How do you like me now?
jokes.setup: Meredith reveals her completely shaved head
jokes.punchline: Her proud, almost sexy presentation of her baldness
jokes.explanation: Great visual reveal with Meredith's characteristic confidence despite the shocking appearance

jokes.text: I'm the only one with the balls to show them lice who's boss.
jokes.setup: Everyone reacting negatively to Meredith's bald head
jokes.punchline: Her defiant pride in her extreme solution
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Meredith's twisted pride in her extreme measures

jokes.text: You look like a baby who suddenly aged 50 years. A cute baby, but something sucked the life force out of it.
jokes.setup: Pam trying to compliment Meredith's bald look
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly specific and disturbing description
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific visual description that gets worse the more detailed it becomes

jokes.text: I'll take a pack of Nicorette gum and a pack of Kools.
jokes.setup: Pam offering to buy Meredith something nice from the store
jokes.punchline: Meredith's contradictory cigarette-related requests
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith contradiction - quitting aids and cigarettes simultaneously

jokes.text: Let's talk pubes, people.
jokes.setup: The office dealing with lice situation
jokes.punchline: Dwight's blunt transition to discussing pubic lice
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight inappropriate directness delivered with business-like efficiency

jokes.text: Oh, that was like a car crash. I couldn't look away.
jokes.setup: Darryl and Val's awkward interaction
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's brutal but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Solid observational comment that captures the awkwardness

jokes.text: This won't help him. It's a muffin, not cake.
jokes.setup: Kevin wanting to make Darryl chocolate cake for comfort
jokes.punchline: His rejection of the muffin for being technically incorrect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic - pedantically correct about comfort food while missing the point

jokes.text: Jim making the shot followed by Pam's mayo disaster timing
jokes.setup: Jim having a great time playing basketball with Dr. J
jokes.punchline: Cut to Pam's mayonnaise exploding everywhere at the exact moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic juxtaposition between Jim's success and Pam's disaster

jokes.text: No! No! No!
jokes.setup: Pam's mayonnaise jar breaking in her hands
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly desperate denials as the situation worsens
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation that works through Pam's desperation

jokes.text: Oh, God. Stuck with the weirdo.
jokes.setup: Creed being paired with someone for lice treatment
jokes.punchline: Creed calling someone else the weirdo
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - the weirdest character calling someone else weird

jokes.text: You don't want bugs, you know? Who knows where those bugs will end up.
jokes.setup: Angela getting aggressive lice treatment from Meredith
jokes.punchline: Her paranoid concerns about lice migration
jokes.explanation: Angela's typical germaphobia taken to absurd levels

jokes.text: Eiffel tower.
jokes.setup: Erin and Pete doing each other's mayonnaise hair treatment
jokes.punchline: Their innocent comment about the hair formation
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo that works because of the characters' innocence

jokes.text: The men dry up, and the nights get lonely. The only calls on your machine are from collection agencies about that pair of motorcycle boots you've never even worn.
jokes.setup: Angela trying to convince Val to take Darryl back
jokes.punchline: Her oddly specific and personal-sounding warnings about single life
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific details that sound suspiciously autobiographical from prim Angela

jokes.text: At your feet, a dying bird. But where did it come from? Why did you kill it? It is because, in some strange way, it is you?
jokes.setup: Angela's warnings about single life getting increasingly dramatic
jokes.punchline: The completely surreal bird metaphor
jokes.explanation: Absolutely bizarre and poetic escalation that's both profound and nonsensical, peak weird Angela

jokes.text: what would you say to you and me hitting the town? 'Cause I'm free, literally, forever.
jokes.setup: Kevin asking Val out after she rejected taking Darryl back
jokes.punchline: His depressing admission of permanent availability
jokes.explanation: Perfectly sad Kevin moment - trying to be smooth but revealing his loneliness

jokes.text: I was kind of feeling good about re-entering the dating pool, and then Kevin asked me out. Thought I might trade up to a new level of man. Then Kevin asked me out.
jokes.setup: Val explaining her confidence about dating again
jokes.punchline: The repeated devastation of Kevin's romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Good repetition structure showing how Kevin's interest killed her confidence twice

jokes.text: Shave her head! Shave her head!
jokes.setup: Pam learning her daughter still has lice
jokes.punchline: Dwight's immediate extreme solution
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight extremism - immediately jumping to the most drastic solution

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, princess fancy-pants letting Jane 12-pack over here take the fall!
jokes.setup: Meredith being angry about taking blame for lice
jokes.punchline: Her creative insults for Pam and herself
jokes.explanation: Creative Meredith nicknames that are both self-deprecating and accusatory

jokes.text: Sure, I gave everybody pink-eye once, and my ex keyed a few of their cars. And, yeah, I BMed in the Shredder on New Year's, but I didn't bring the lice in.
jokes.setup: Meredith defending herself against blame
jokes.punchline: Her escalating list of actual workplace violations
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating list of increasingly horrifying Meredith violations, ending with the shredder incident

jokes.text: Well, the * effect that I don't think has kicked in yet. I'm gonna count down for ten. Nine... Yellow... Cold. Purple.
jokes.setup: Dwight setting off an insecticide grenade and getting affected
jokes.punchline: His countdown deteriorating into random words as he's poisoned
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy showing Dwight's overconfidence backfiring in real time

jokes.text: Wow. He got to purple.
jokes.setup: Dwight's poisoning countdown ending at 'purple'
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact assessment of Dwight's condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim understatement treating Dwight's bizarre behavior as mildly interesting

jokes.text: You're waterboarding me!
jokes.setup: Someone washing mayonnaise out of Dwight's hair
jokes.punchline: His dramatic comparison to torture techniques
jokes.explanation: Typical Dwight overdramatization of a mundane situation

jokes.text: You do kind of look like Elvis, but we should probably wash all the dead lice out.
jokes.setup: Pam commenting on Meredith's mayo-covered hair style
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of dead lice mixed with the Elvis compliment
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of compliment with gross reality

jokes.text: I wonder what happened over there. I've been sitting here the whole time.
jokes.setup: Loud destruction sounds from the warehouse
jokes.punchline: Creed's suspicious denial despite being in a different location
jokes.explanation: Classic Creed suspicious behavior implying guilt while maintaining innocence

jokes.text: I got the bartender's phone number when you were in the john. I'm gonna take that freak to bone town before the night is over.
jokes.setup: Meredith and Pam having drinks together
jokes.punchline: Meredith's crude confidence about her romantic prospects while bald
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith crudeness showing her unshakeable confidence despite looking terrible

tmdb_overview: Pam accidentally brings lice into the office and lets Meredith take the fall, while Dwight vows to destroy the parasites. Meanwhile, Jim has a fantastic day in Philly wooing a potential business associate. Meanwhile, Phyllis, Nellie and Kevin meddle in Darryl's love life.
